unified_dashboard_question.question: The "Unified" name implies cross-account aggregation. Users unfamiliar with multi-account setups may not understand what "Unified" means.

event_attribution_placement.decision: Placed in Analytics alongside Revenue Attribution. Both configure campaign measurement rules and are conceptually paired.

event_attribution_question.question: SAP docs place this under Email > Reporting. While it configures rules rather than displaying reports, users associate attribution with analytics. Consider whether "Conversion Analytics" (Peti's suggestion) better describes the feature.

content_design_placement.decision: Placed under Content, aligned with SAP docs' Brand Center categorization.

content_design_question.question: Tree test showed low success (26%) — users split between Content and Channels. The "design" framing confuses users who think channel-first (e.g., "I want to design my Web Channel themes"). Consider whether clearer naming would help.

mediadb_brand_center_question.question: Currently opens in a popup modal, not as a full page. Consider if this should remain a menu item or be accessed from within content editors.

templates_placement.decision: Placed under Content for convenience, though SAP docs place it under Email.

templates_question.question: SAP documentation clearly categorizes Email Templates under Email, not Content. The navigation back-link to Email Campaigns confirms functional coupling. The rename to "Block-Based Email Templates" (per Peti/TillyG alignment) clarifies the email-specific scope.

personalization_0_placement.decision: Placed under Content — personalization rules are a content creation tool, used while building campaigns.

personalization_0_question.question: SAP describes this as an omnichannel capability, broader than just email content. The feature includes personalization tokens, scripting language, and conditional content rules. Does it deserve more prominence than a Content sub-item?

aim_coupon_pools_placement.decision: Placed under Content for ease of access during campaign creation.

aim_coupon_pools_question.question: SAP officially categorizes Vouchers under Personalization, not Content. If Personalization becomes a separate group, Vouchers should move with it. The "Omnichannel" prefix indicates cross-channel use beyond just email.

custdev_icalendar_configurator_placement.decision: Placed in Data & Integrations as an integration tool between the platform and external calendar systems.

custdev_icalendar_configurator_question.question: Very niche feature with no analytics data. Consider whether it needs a standalone menu item at all, or could be a setting within Email configuration.

link_categories_placement.decision: Placement varies — either near Email Campaigns in Channels or in Administration as a low-traffic configuration.

link_categories_question.question: SAP docs categorize this under Email > Links. With the "Email" prefix rename, the scope is clear regardless of placement. The key question: is this accessed during campaign creation (Channels) or during initial setup (Administration)?

bounce_mail_management_placement.decision: Dual-purpose feature: both an operational contact management tool AND an email deliverability setting.

bounce_mail_management_question.question: SAP docs place this under Email > Deliverability, but the menu access path is Contacts > Bounce Management. The feature serves two audiences: campaign ops (checking bounces daily) and admins (configuring thresholds once). Should placement follow the docs (Email/Channels) or the operational pattern (Contacts/Audience)?

frequency_cap_placement.decision: Placement varies — either in Administration (SAP docs support: General Settings) or in Channels (proximity to email campaigns).

frequency_cap_question.question: SAP docs explicitly categorize this under General Settings (account-level), not under Email or Channels. This is the strongest evidence for Administration/Account Management placement.

internal_blacklist_placement.decision: Placed near email operations due to high usage and email-specific scope.

internal_blacklist_question.question: At 6,048 UPV this is more actively used than most features. SAP docs place it under Email > Deliverability. The high traffic suggests daily operational use by campaign teams, not occasional admin access.

mobile_channel_question.question: SAP docs use "Mobile Engage" as the feature name, which encompasses push notifications, in-app messages, and app inbox. The name implies broader mobile engagement beyond just push.

sms_channel_question.question: SAP docs title is "SMS and MMS - Overview". The menu name "SMS Campaigns" focuses on the campaign aspect. Peti suggests "SMS Channel" to align with other channel naming patterns.

sms_settings_placement.decision: Placed near SMS Campaigns in Channels, or in Administration for low-traffic variants.

sms_settings_question.question: EME guidelines recommend merging SMS Settings as a tab within the SMS Campaigns page (requires development). Until then, proximity to SMS Campaigns is the closest menu-level approximation.

conversation_placement.decision: Placed in Channels. SAP official name is "Conversational Channel" (singular).

conversation_question.question: SAP docs use "Conversational Channel" (singular). Peti and TillyG both suggest "Conversational Channels" (plural) since multiple messaging platforms are under this umbrella. The plural form is technically more accurate.

web_push_question.question: Web Push and Web Channel are both under "Web" in SAP docs but serve different purposes. Web Push is a notification channel; Web Channel is on-site personalization. Keeping them together under "Web" or separating them is a design choice.

web_channel_question.question: Web Channel is fundamentally different from other "channels" — it's website personalization, not message sending. This distinction may affect whether it belongs with messaging channels or with content/personalization tools.

embedded_messaging_question.question: Embedded Messaging spans both mobile (app inbox) and web (site inbox). Very low usage (104 UPV). Consider if this needs standalone menu visibility.

segments_placement.decision: Placed in the Audience/Contacts group. The second most-used feature (123K UPV), foundational to all targeted marketing.

contacts_page_placement.decision: Placed in the Audience/Contacts group. Page needs to be created as a new feature.

consent_placement.decision: Placed in the Audience/Contacts group, renamed to "Consent Management."

consent_question.question: SAP docs place Consent under Mobile Engage (mobile app consent). This feature may be newly introduced for the Enterprise Edition. Verify: (1) Is this Enterprise-only? (2) Does it cover all-channel consent or just mobile? (3) If new, it may not have analytics data from the classic edition.

self_service_sso_question.question: Marked for removal. Low usage (821 UPV). May be replaced by organization-level SSO configuration outside the product.

security_settings_placement.decision: Placed in Administration, renamed to "API Credentials" to accurately reflect current page content.

security_settings_question.question: The "Security Settings" name implies broader scope (password policies, 2FA, session management) but the page currently only manages API credentials. Renaming to "API Credentials" is accurate but limits future expansion if security features are added.

sdk_management_placement.decision: Placed in Administration as a technical setup tool.

sdk_management_question.question: Very low usage (448 UPV) after initial setup. Primarily used by developers during integration, not by marketers. Consider whether this needs prominent menu placement or could be in a technical settings area.

context_access_manager_placement.decision: Placement varies — Content (tree test data supports) or Administration (SAP docs support).

context_access_manager_question.question: Tags are genuinely cross-domain — they apply to emails, automations, segments, and contacts. SAP places it in Account Administration; tree tests show users look in Content. Neither placement is perfect. The question is: do users think of tags as "organizing my content" or "platform configuration"?

integration_monitor_question.question: Clearly a data monitoring/debugging tool. Fits well in Data & Integrations group.

engagement_events_placement.decision: Placed in Data & Integrations as an integration configuration tool.

engagement_events_question.question: SAP docs categorize Engagement Events under Automation > Triggers, suggesting closer proximity to Automation Programs. However, the configuration involves integrating external event sources (APIs, commerce platforms), which is a data integration task.

revenue_attributor_placement.decision: Placed in Analytics alongside Event Attribution. SAP documentation supports this — both are campaign measurement configurations.

revenue_attributor_question.question: Should attribution configuration live with the reports it feeds, or with the settings that rarely change? The SAP docs' placement under Reporting suggests the former.

web_extend_placement.decision: Placed in Data & Integrations. Web Extend is a data collection tool, not a marketing feature.

universal_schema_builder_question.question: A data integration tool — connects external data sources. Clearly belongs in Data & Integrations or Data Management.

automation_webhook_node_question.question: An automation tool that integrates with external systems. Could belong with Automation or with Data & Integrations depending on whether you prioritize the automation workflow context or the integration aspect.

spc_data_quality_question.question: Renamed from "Product Catalog Data" to "Product Catalog" per PM + research confirmation. The shorter name is clearer — "Data" was redundant since the page is already in a data-focused group.

group_analytics_groupname.question: What should the "Analytics" group be called? Different variants use different names.

group_content_groupname.question: What should the "Content" group be called? Different variants use different names.

group_contacts_groupname.question: What should the "Contacts" group be called? Different variants use different names.

group_management_groupname.question: What should the "Management" group be called? Different variants use different names.
